# checks.base.Check { #pycmdcheck.checks.base.Check }

```python
checks.base.Check()
```

Protocol defining the interface for all checks.

This protocol defines what a check must implement to be usable
by pycmdcheck. Checks can be implemented by either:

1. Inheriting from BaseCheck (recommended for most cases)
2. Implementing this Protocol directly (for structural subtyping)

## Attributes {.doc-section .doc-section-attributes}

<code>[**name**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Unique identifier for the check (e.g., "metadata", "tests").

<code>[**description**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Human-readable description of what the check does.

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

Implement a check using the protocol directly:

```python
>>> class MyCheck:
...     name = "my_check"
...     description = "My custom check"
...
...     def run(self, package_path, config):
...         return CheckResult(
...             name=self.name,
...             status=CheckStatus.OK,
...             message="Check passed",
...         )
>>> isinstance(MyCheck(), Check)
True
```

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [run](#pycmdcheck.checks.base.Check.run) | Execute the check and return result. |

### run { #pycmdcheck.checks.base.Check.run }

```python
checks.base.Check.run(package_path, config)
```

Execute the check and return result.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**package_path**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[Path](`pathlib.Path`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Path to the package directory to check.

<code>[**config**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[dict](`dict`)\[[str](`str`), [Any](`typing.Any`)\]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Configuration dictionary for this check, loaded from pyproject.toml [tool.pycmdcheck.checks.<name>] section.

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[CheckResult](`pycmdcheck.results.CheckResult`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   A CheckResult containing the status, message, and optional details.
